title: SARS -> Temporary Asia Freight Demand Slump
summary: The SARS outbreak hit Asia travel and trade sentiment, briefly affecting freight demand and logistics confidence.
mechanism: Health shocks can temporarily dent trade flows and logistics activity before sharp normalization.
causal_chain: health scare reduces mobility and trade confidence
shipping demand softens
freight markets weaken before recovery

forecast.note: Bearish freight demand during acute outbreak phase.

outcome.realized_note: Freight softness proved temporary as trade activity normalized.
outcome.reversal_note: Recovery followed quickly after outbreak control.

title: Global Financial Crisis -> Baltic Dry Index Crash
summary: As trade finance and commodity demand collapsed in 2008, the Baltic Dry Index plunged dramatically.
mechanism: A sudden drop in commodity trade and financing caused dry-bulk shipping demand to collapse faster than vessel supply could adjust.
causal_chain: credit crisis chokes trade finance
commodity cargo demand collapses
dry-bulk freight rates crash

forecast.note: Bearish freight and bulk shipping equities.

outcome.realized_note: The Baltic Dry Index collapsed in one of the sharpest freight drawdowns on record.
outcome.reversal_note: Partial rebound came with stimulus and trade normalization.

title: Eyjafjallajokull -> Air Cargo and Perishable Supply Shock
summary: The Iceland volcanic eruption shut much of European airspace, disrupting air cargo, perishables, and time-sensitive logistics.
mechanism: Airspace closures break time-sensitive supply chains quickly, especially in perishables and just-in-time goods.
causal_chain: volcanic ash closes major airspace
air cargo capacity disappears
perishable logistics and spot rates dislocate

forecast.note: Bullish spot cargo rates, bearish perishables throughput.

outcome.realized_note: Air-cargo disruption caused immediate shortages and logistics dislocation across Europe.

title: US West Coast Port Congestion -> Import Supply Delays
summary: Labor tension and congestion at US West Coast ports delayed imports and tightened logistics capacity.
mechanism: Port bottlenecks delay cargo release, increasing transit times and raising end-to-end logistics costs.
causal_chain: port congestion and labor issues slow throughput
inventory arrivals are delayed
spot logistics costs rise and supply chains tighten

forecast.note: Bullish logistics costs, bearish inventory availability.

outcome.realized_note: Import delays and supply-chain friction increased materially during the congestion episode.
outcome.reversal_note: Resolved after labor agreement and easing congestion.

title: Hanjin Bankruptcy -> Container Logistics Shock
summary: Hanjin Shipping's collapse stranded cargo and tightened available container logistics capacity.
mechanism: A large carrier failure strands vessels and cargo, reducing usable capacity and creating operational chaos.
causal_chain: major carrier enters bankruptcy
cargo becomes stranded and ships are denied services
spot rates and logistics premiums rise

forecast.note: Bullish spot rates on operational capacity loss.

outcome.realized_note: Container logistics tightened and cargo disruption spread globally after Hanjin failed.
outcome.reversal_note: Effects faded as cargo was reallocated.

title: Hurricane Harvey -> Product Shipping and Port Disruption
summary: Hurricane Harvey disrupted Gulf Coast refining, ports, and product logistics, affecting tanker and refined-product flows.
mechanism: Refinery and port outages reroute product flows and tighten localized shipping/logistics capacity.
causal_chain: storm shuts refining and ports
product flows reroute and inventories dislocate
shipping and gasoline logistics reprice

forecast.note: Bullish refined-product freight and crack spreads.

outcome.realized_note: Product distribution and Gulf logistics were heavily disrupted after Harvey.

title: COVID Shock -> Container Collapse Then Explosive Freight Spike
summary: Container logistics first collapsed during early COVID demand shutdowns, then entered historic shortage as goods demand rebounded.
mechanism: A sharp collapse in network synchronization left equipment and port capacity misallocated just as goods demand later surged.
causal_chain: COVID shuts demand and disrupts transport networks
containers and port capacity become mispositioned
goods rebound causes historic freight rate spike

forecast.note: Bullish freight after the first reopening wave.

outcome.realized_note: Global container rates reached historic highs in 2021 due to severe dislocation.

title: Yantian Port Shutdown -> Container Rate Spike
summary: COVID restrictions at Yantian port in Shenzhen caused major container delays and tightened Asia-Europe/US rates.
mechanism: A major export port shutdown reduces throughput and amplifies already-stressed container bottlenecks.
causal_chain: COVID restrictions cut port throughput
container backlog builds
spot rates rise as capacity tightens further

outcome.realized_note: Yantian delays worsened the 2021 container crunch and supported higher spot freight.

title: LA/Long Beach Congestion -> US Retail Logistics Stress
summary: Historic queues outside LA/Long Beach amplified US import delays and freight dislocation in 2021.
mechanism: Port congestion cascades inland, increasing end-to-end delays and keeping equipment out of circulation.
causal_chain: record import surge hits congested ports
vessels queue and inland logistics clog
import delays and logistics costs remain elevated

outcome.realized_note: The port queues became emblematic of 2021 supply-chain inflation.
outcome.reversal_note: Queue lengths and rates fell later as demand normalized.

title: Ever Given Aftershock -> Schedule Reliability Collapse
summary: After the Ever Given blockage, container schedule reliability deteriorated further as global networks absorbed delays.
mechanism: Even after a chokepoint reopens, delayed vessel rotation can keep logistics systems impaired for weeks.
causal_chain: vessel bunching follows canal reopening
schedule reliability collapses
container rates and delays stay elevated

outcome.realized_note: Network reliability remained poor long after the canal physically reopened.

title: Russia-Ukraine War -> Black Sea Shipping Insurance Spike
summary: The invasion of Ukraine caused war-risk insurance costs and vessel routing stress across the Black Sea.
mechanism: War risk reprices insurance, crew availability, and route viability, effectively tightening usable shipping capacity.
causal_chain: war begins in a major export basin
insurance and routing costs jump
freight and commodity export channels dislocate

forecast.note: Bullish war-risk freight and insurance costs.

outcome.realized_note: Black Sea logistics and war-risk premiums spiked sharply in early 2022.
outcome.reversal_note: Partial easing came under grain-corridor agreements.

title: Panama Canal Drought -> Transit Cuts and Route Repricing
summary: Drought reduced Panama Canal transits and draft, forcing rerouting and longer voyage times.
mechanism: Transit limits reduce effective shipping capacity by increasing queuing and forcing longer alternate routes.
causal_chain: drought lowers canal water availability
transit slots and draft are cut
route times rise and freight markets tighten

forecast.note: Bullish freight via reduced effective capacity.

outcome.realized_note: Canal constraints materially altered routing and increased shipping costs.
outcome.reversal_note: Relief depends on rainfall and slot normalization.

title: Red Sea Attacks -> Container and Tanker Rerouting
summary: Attacks in the Red Sea forced carriers to reroute around the Cape of Good Hope, tightening global shipping capacity.
mechanism: Longer routes reduce effective fleet supply and raise insurance and fuel costs simultaneously.
causal_chain: attacks raise transit risk
carriers reroute around Africa
voyage times lengthen and effective capacity shrinks

forecast.note: Bullish container and tanker rates on rerouting.

outcome.realized_note: Freight rates and insurance rose as carriers rerouted around the Cape.
outcome.reversal_note: Rates can normalize quickly if security improves.

title: IMO Emissions Rules -> Slow Steaming and Effective Capacity Reduction
summary: New emissions-compliance behavior encouraged slow steaming and reduced effective shipping capacity in parts of the fleet.
mechanism: Environmental compliance can cut effective transport supply by reducing average vessel speed.
causal_chain: new rules incentivize lower operating speeds
voyage duration increases
effective shipping capacity falls and supports rates

forecast.note: Bullish freight at the margin via capacity reduction.

outcome.realized_note: The effect was gradual but supportive for rate floors and asset values in constrained segments.

sources.title: IMO Carbon Rules and Shipping Capacity
